title: Diplazium chattagramicum
taxonomy: Diplazium chattagramicum is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It is a type of deciduous fern that belongs to the order Polypodiales. The plant is a vascular epiphyte, meaning it can grow on other plants. This species is native to the tropical regions of the Indian subcontinent. Diplazium chattagramicum is further classified into the genus Diplazium, which is a group of vascular plants commonly known as the wood ferns.
uses: Diplazium chattagramicum has been found to possess various medicinal properties. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. More studies are required to fully understand its potential medicinal uses and benefits.
